title: Etymology and Ancient Origins
focus: Explores the legendary origins of Lucknow (Lakshmanapuri) and its place in the context of the 16 Mahajanapadas.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 046]
The Origins of a Name
Etymology
"Lucknow" is the anglicised spelling of the local pronunciation "Lakhnau". According to legend, the city is named after Lakshmana, a hero of the Hindu epic Ramayana. The legend states that Lakshmana had a palace or estate in the area, called Lakshmanapuri (Lakshmana's city).
Evolution
The settlement came to be known as Lakhanpur (or Lachhmanpur) by the 11th century, and later evolved into Lucknow.

[Page 047]
Ancient Time - Lucknow
India, 600 B.C.: The 16 Mahajanapadas
The map illustrates the locations of the 16 Mahajanapadas and major cities during the 6th century B.C. in the Indian subcontinent.
Mahajanapadas and Cities:
- Kamboja
- Gandhara
- Kuru (Cities: Hatthipura, Indraprashta)
- Panchala
- Kosala
- Shurasena
- Matsya
- Malla
- Vrijji (City: Pataliputra)
- Kashi (City: Baranasi)
- Anga
- Magadha (City: Rajagaha)
- Vatsa
- Chetiya
- Avanti
- Assaka
